address.full: 2921-2923 El Cajon Boulevard, San Diego, CA 92104

site.legal_description: Block 119, Lots 7 & 8, University Heights D'Hemecourt Map (Amended), City of San Diego, County of San Diego, State of California

project.title: Change of Occupancy — Rear Warehouse to R-3 Townhouses, 2921-2923 El Cajon Blvd
project.scope: Interior change of occupancy and remodel of the existing rear warehouse shell at APN 446-232-04-00 (Lots 7 & 8, Block 119, 7,039 SF total). Rear shell (~46' wide × ~40' deep, ~22 ft tall, existing flat roof) fully retained — no exterior demolition, no new footprint. Interior work only: new structural floor deck at ~11 ft AFF creates 2 residential floors within the existing shell (10-ft ground floor + 1-ft floor assembly + 10-ft 2nd floor = 22 ft total, matching existing shell height exactly). Proposed program: 2 Group R-3 townhouse units (CBC 310.5), each 2-story (~20' wide × 40' deep per floor), separated by a single CBC 706 structurally-independent fire wall running continuously from foundation through new floor deck to existing flat roof plane. No shared corridor — each unit has a private internal staircase and private ground-floor exit directly to the rear alley. Upper-floor sleeping rooms: CBC 1030 emergency escape windows facing rear alley (subject to DS-375 Q5 confirmation). GBA of converted area: ~3,680 SF (1,840 SF/floor × 2 stories). R-3 classification via fire wall path avoids NFPA 13R sprinkler requirement — CBC 903.2.8 does not apply to R-3. Existing 4,550 SF + 1,910 SF mezzanine Group B commercial (Digital Gym Media Arts Center) at front of building RETAINED — no change to exterior, occupancy, or tenant. Zero parking (TPA). Rear alley access. SB 330 Preliminary Application filed concurrently.

existing.sf: ~6,460 SF total (4,550 SF + 1,910 SF mezzanine, Group B — RETAINED); rear warehouse shell ~1,840 SF/floor
existing.stories: 1 + mezzanine (front Group B); 1-story warehouse shell at rear (~22 ft to flat roof)
existing.occupancy: B (Digital Gym front + warehouse rear — both Group B currently)

proposed.sf: ~3,680 SF GBA (1,840 SF/floor × 2 stories, interior conversion of existing rear shell)
proposed.stories: 2 (new interior floor deck within existing 22-ft shell — no change to exterior height)
proposed.construction_type: V-B (existing exterior shell retained; new interior floor framing and CBC 706 fire wall V-B)
proposed.occupancy: B (front — Digital Gym retained) + R-3 (rear — 2 townhouse units via COO, ~3,680 SF GBA)
proposed.use: mixed (existing B front unchanged + new R-3 rear conversion)

project.deviations: None anticipated. No exterior modifications proposed. Existing shell height (~22 ft) unchanged. R-3 fire wall path (CBC 310.5 + CBC 706) is the design basis — CBC 903.2.8 sprinkler requirement does not apply to R-3.
project.earthwork_quantities: No grading — interior work only within existing building shell. No new footprint.
project.public_improvements: None anticipated from interior-only COO. El Cajon Blvd frontage improvements TBD per DS-375 Q10 response.

ds375.policy_questions:
Q1 [Building Plan Review — R-3 Classification via CBC 706 Fire Wall] Per CBC 310.5, if the 2 dwelling units within the rear warehouse conversion are each 2-story townhouses separated by a single CBC 706 fire wall (structurally independent, continuous from foundation through the new interior floor deck to the existing flat roof plane), may each unit be classified as Group R-3, thereby removing the CBC 903.2.8 NFPA 13R automatic sprinkler requirement for the conversion?

Q2 [Building Plan Review — CBC 706 Fire Wall in Existing Shell] Per CBC 706.4, a fire wall must be structurally independent. For a new interior fire wall constructed within an existing ~22-ft warehouse shell — where the wall connects to the existing exterior walls at the perimeter and bears on a new interior footing — what structural independence detail does DSD require to satisfy CBC 706.4 in an existing-building change-of-occupancy context?

Q3 [Building Plan Review — COO Compliance Path] Under IEBC Section 1001.2, what compliance path (prescriptive, work area, or performance) does DSD require for converting a Group B warehouse rear shell to 2 Group R-3 townhouse units via interior-only work, with the existing exterior shell fully retained and no structural modification to exterior walls or roof?

Q4 [Building Plan Review — Occupancy Separation B/R-3 Interface] Per CBC 508.4, the retained front portion is Group B (Digital Gym) and the converted rear is Group R-3. What occupancy separation assembly is required at the interface between the existing Group B front area and the new Group R-3 rear area — specifically at (a) the ground-level covered underpass zone (~6 ft clear, belonging to the front B structure) and (b) the upper level where the front building's flat roof (~12 ft AFF) abuts the rear warehouse wall?

Q5 [Building Plan Review — Emergency Escape, 2nd Floor Sleeping Rooms] Per CBC 1030.1, each sleeping room above the first floor in a non-sprinklered R-3 building requires an emergency escape and rescue opening. The 2nd floor of each townhouse unit (floor deck at ~11 ft AFF) is approximately level with the adjacent front Group B building's flat roof (~12 ft AFF). May the required CBC 1030 emergency escape openings face the front building's flat roof, given that the roof is accessible from grade via existing roof-access inside the Group B building? Or must openings face directly to a yard or public way (e.g., rear alley side)?

Q6 [Building Plan Review — Structural: New Floor Deck in Existing Shell] Per IEBC Section 807 and ASCE 7, does inserting a new structural floor deck at ~11 ft AFF inside an existing ~22-ft warehouse shell — bearing on the existing exterior walls — require a structural analysis of the existing wall framing for the new tributary floor loads, and is a special inspection program required for the new floor-to-wall bearing connections?

Q7 [Building Plan Review — Accessibility, COO] Per IEBC 705 and CBC 11B-202.4, does a change of occupancy from Group B to Group R-3 (2 townhouse units) in an existing building at APN 446-232-04-00 trigger path-of-travel upgrade requirements to the building entrance or accessible route, and does the 20% valuation cap under IEBC 705.1.1 apply to limit the scope of required upgrades?

Q8 [Planning — CCHS/TAOZ and Density] Per SDMC 131.0436 (CC-3-9, 1 DU/400 SF base) and SDMC 131.0460 (CCHS Tier 3, 1 DU/200 SF), for an interior COO creating 2 Group R-3 townhouse units with no new exterior footprint at APN 446-232-04-00: (a) does the density formula apply to the converted GBA (~3,680 SF), the total lot SF (7,039 SF), or another measure; and (b) does the COO path qualify for CCHS Tier 3 incentives, or is density limited to the CC-3-9 base standard?

Q9 [Land Development Review — Inclusionary Housing] Under SDMC 142.1301, for 2 net-new Group R-3 units created by COO in an existing building at APN 446-232-04-00 in North Park, is the IHO in-lieu fee obligation triggered at 2 units, or only at a higher unit-count threshold?

Q10 [Engineering — Right-of-Way] Per SDMC 144.0130 and City Council Policy 600-37 (Complete Streets), does an interior-only change of occupancy at APN 446-232-04-00 with El Cajon Blvd frontage and no exterior construction activity trigger street improvement, sidewalk widening, or bicycle infrastructure requirements as a condition of building permit issuance?
